pipeline_status.curmudgeon: Cycle 2, Phase 1: 12/67 WINs reviewed (WIN-001 through WIN-012). Last reviewed: WIN-012 at 2026-04-08T01:15Z. Verdict held (Self-Contradicted confirmed, confidence 0.89).
pipeline_status.decider: 8 patch files awaiting human application (see status.json). No new critical infrastructure issues. Digest shows 0 unprocessed Cycle 2 reviews in queue (note: script bug — WIN-011.c2 and WIN-012.c2 were treated as processed due to Cycle 1 legacy ID matching; manually processed this run and added to ledger).

pipeline_health_notes: DIGEST SCRIPT BUG (low priority): digest-reviews.js treats WIN-NNN.c2.json as 'already processed' if WIN-NNN.json (Cycle 1) was processed, due to the processedWinIds legacy-format fallback. This means new Cycle 2 reviews are invisible to the digest. Workaround: decider should manually check for .c2.json files not yet in processed-reviews.json. Bug reported here for tinker agent to fix.
EXP-007 and EXP-011 remain complete but unintegrated (require manual HTML scaffold / build-doc-v4.js changes). These are infrastructure tasks for a human developer session, not agent-patchable.

external_changes.dome_site_changes: No substantive changes. Automated commits only (monitor.py every 60-90 min, pull_data.py every 6h). V51.0 active.

internal_issues.description: Spatial heterogeneity loophole: dome can argue eclipse gravity anomalies are location-dependent (Mohe 1997 +6.5 uGal, Membach 1999 0.0 uGal, China SG 2009 0.0 uGal). Our internal contradiction argument does not address this rescue hypothesis. Need to show: (1) dome never specified which locations should see effects; (2) post-hoc heterogeneity is unfalsifiable; (3) kappa depends on a single contested measurement.

Misleading 'two independent eclipse events' framing: kappa numerator (-10.9 nT, BOU 2017, magnetic only) and denominator (-6.5 uGal, Mohe 1997, gravity only) from different eclipses at different locations. No single eclipse measured both fields simultaneously.

suggested_patch.find: The derivation is also circular: assume the Mohe anomaly is real → calculate κ → claim κ as a confirmed parameter.
suggested_patch.replace: See suggested-patches-2026-04-08T08-00.json — adds clarification about BOU 2017/Mohe 1997 measurement independence and ratio stability argument.

internal_issues.description: Ratio stability argument not addressed: dome defender can argue 'if kappa is real, it should be stable across eclipses.' Review needs to explain why this stability is illusory when numerator and denominator come from different experiments.
Stray GRACE L1A sentence in WIN-011 detail_extra. GRACE is unrelated to eclipse ground gravimetry. Carryover from parked Section 3.6.

suggested_patch.find: The GRACE L1A claim conflates atmospheric drag with gravitational signal.

internal_issues.description: CHAOS-7 DOI still wrong (01163-9 should be 01252-9). Patch exists in suggested-patches-2026-04-07T15-30.json awaiting application.
WIN-005 monitoring tag incorrectly set to 'live_fetch'; should be 'hardcoded'. Patch in suggested-patches-2026-04-08T06-30.json awaiting application.

recommended_actions.action: Apply the 8 pending patch files in chronological order. Run 'node build-scripts/apply-patches.js <file>' for each, then 'node build.js html && node test.js' after each batch. Critical patches: ISS-490 (WIN-005 CHAOS-7 DOI), ISS-491 (WIN-005 monitoring tag), ISS-494 (WIN-054 verdict chain).

Fix digest-reviews.js bug: the processedWinIds legacy fallback causes Cycle 2 reviews to be silently treated as processed if the Cycle 1 review was processed. Add cycle-aware processing: if a .c2.json file exists but is not in processedSet directly (by filename), treat it as pending regardless of processedWinIds. Assign to tinker agent.
Manual integration of EXP-007 (tab reorder in docs/index.html) and EXP-011 (DOCX parity build-doc-v4.js edits). These require developer session, not agent patches.
Analyst: pick up EXP-014 (WIN-012 spatial heterogeneity loophole). High priority — this is the strongest potential counter-argument to our best Self-Contradicted verdict.
